item.title: Running PostgreSQL in memory with docker
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Introduction

InôÙteôÙracôÙting wiôÙth a daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse, can be a reôÙguôÙlar taôÙsk of a deôÙveôÙloôÙpeôÙr, and for thaôÙt we would like to enôÙsuôÙre that we are deôÙveôÙloôÙping and tesôÙting in siôÙtuaôÙtions cloôÙse ôÙto a real imôÙpleôÙmenôÙtaôÙtioôÙn; theôÙreôÙfoôÙre, using the saôÙme daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse as in ôÙproôÙducôÙtion can help deôÙtecôÙting isôÙsues earôÙlôÙy.

HoôÙweôÙveôÙr, seôÙtting up an enôÙtiôÙre daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse serôÙver for deôÙveôÙloôÙpôÙmenôÙt, can beôÙ ôÙcumôÙberôÙsoôÙme. HoôÙpeôÙfuôÙlly noôÙwaôÙdaôÙys, moôÙdern opeôÙraôÙting sysôÙteôÙms like LiôÙnux haôÙve ôÙgreat tools and feaôÙtuôÙres that we could take adôÙvanôÙtaôÙge of.

In parôÙtiôÙcuôÙlaôÙr, I would like to seôÙtup a simôÙple daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse loôÙcaôÙlly usinôÙg doôÙcker, and stoôÙring the daôÙta in meôÙmoôÙrôÙy.

The idea is simôÙple: run a doôÙcker conôÙtaiôÙner wiôÙth the imaôÙge for PosôÙtgresSôÙQL, uôÙsing a tmôÙpfs [1] [2] as stoôÙraôÙge for the daôÙtaôÙbaôÙse (a raôÙmôÙfs could alôÙsoôÙ ôÙbe usôÙeôÙd).

Leer mûÀsãÎ (quedan 3 minutos de lectura)

item.title: Running RabbitMQ server on Docker
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
If you use RaôÙbbitôÙMQ for deôÙveôÙloôÙpôÙment freôÙquenôÙtlôÙy, soôÙmeôÙtiôÙmes you miôÙght haôÙve founôÙd it uses too muôÙch reôÙsourôÙces (iôÙt's norôÙmal whiôÙle proôÙgraôÙmôÙming to haôÙve a lot of queues or taôÙsks being queued and that makes the CPU usage to spike).

Having RabbitMQ installed on the OS seems the perfect approach for production, but on development I'd rather do something different, in order to isolate the process. I know I could bound it (for example order it not to start automatically as a dûÎmon), by means of systemd but a few weeks ago I decided to try docker and see how it results.

It turôÙned out to be just the tool for the woôÙrk, and so far wiôÙth a liôÙttle simôÙple ôÙconôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtion it can run as exôÙpecôÙteôÙd.

TheôÙre is alôÙready a doôÙcker imaôÙge for RaôÙbbitôÙMôÙQ, whiôÙch can be auôÙtoôÙmaôÙtiôÙcaôÙlly puôÙlleôÙd, and then run, for examôÙple:

sudo docker pull rabbitmq
sudo docker run -d -e RABBITMQ_NODENAME=my-rabbit --cpuset="1" --name docker.rabbitmq -p 5672:5672 rabbitmq:3

The -d option indicates the process to start detached, then by passing -e we pass some environment variables (in this case, the RABBITMQ_NODENAME is a particular variable for rabbit indicating how to set the name of the node it is starting). Optionally, we can limit the CPU usage with the --cpuset, as in this case which sets the process to use the second core of the machine (it starts at 0). Then the --name is a name for the docker being created.

The most important part in this case is the port mapping, made by the -p option which in this case maps the port used by RabbitMQ directly (1:1) with the host machine. This makes the docker process to run transparently, as the other applications that try to communicate with a RabbitMQ won't notice any difference, making it look like is executing an actual RabbitMQ service. Finally there is the name of the docker image to run.

What I usually do is to manage the docker image by its instance_id (a number that is displayed after listing the docker images, by doing sudo docker ps -a). Then we can manage it by sudo docker [start|stop] <instance_id>.

There is another command to see the output being generated by the process which is docker logs rabbitmq.docker. Notice in this case the name designated to the image was used instead of the instance_id. In addition we can see internal data for the process by running the inspect command (again we can use the instance_id or the name we assigned).

docker inspect rabbitmq.docker
sudo docker logs docker.rabbitmq

It's important to notice that docker is actually not a virtualization platform, but a mechanism that runs processes in containers, meaning that in this case the entire RabbitMQ is running as a single process within a container, with some other limitations and bounds constrained by docker.

I found this approôÙach to be veôÙry verôÙsatiôÙle for a deôÙveôÙloôÙpôÙment enôÙviôÙronôÙmenôÙt, and wiôÙth RaôÙbbitôÙMQ being the first piôÙloôÙt, I thiôÙnk I can miôÙgraôÙte moôÙre appliôÙcaôÙtions to doôÙcker insôÙtead of haôÙving them insôÙtaôÙlled on the sysôÙtem (as long as poôÙsôÙsiôÙble).
